validation_rules: If totals exist: total ~= subtotal + tax
If line items exist: total ~= sum(line_items.amount)
instructions:
Extraction Instructions:

- Utility Name: Extract from document header or remittance address block. Anchor with terms like "Eversource", "Utility", or address following "make check payable". Normalize spacing and case.
- Account Number: Anchor with any variant ("Account Number", "Acct #", etc.); value will follow or appear nearby (typically a 10-14 digit grouping). Use the number near payment details or customer block. Take spaces as-is.
- Meter Number: Find section labeled "Meter Number" or "Meter". Extract the adjacent numeric value (may be 8-12 digits).
- Tariff: Look for "Tariff", "Rate", or "DISTRIBUTION RATE:". Normalize to numeric (e.g., "030" becomes 30.0).
- Month: Anchor with "Month", "Billing Month", or explicit monthly statement; if not present, return as NaN.
- Service from: Anchor with "Service from" and extract the date. Accept patterns like "MM/DD/YY" or "MM/DD/YYYY" and normalize to "MM/DD/YY".
- Service to: Anchor with "Service to" or extract second date in "Service from [date] - [date]" strings. Normalize to "MM/DD/YY".
- Duration (Days): Anchor with "Duration", "Days", or text like "31 Days". If not found, return as NaN.
- Electric Energy - Off Peak (kWh): Search for section/table with electric usage by period. Look for label "Off Peak (kWh)" and capture adjacent value. If not found, return NaN.
- Electric Energy - Intermediate Peak (kWh): As above, anchor to "Intermediate Peak (kWh)".
- Electric Energy - On Peak (kWh): As above, anchor to "On Peak (kWh)".
- Total (kWh): Anchor with "Total (kWh)", "Total Usage", or sum for the period. If ambiguous, return NaN.
- Demand - Off Peak (kW): Anchor with "Off Peak (kW)" near "Demand". If not found, return NaN.
- Demand - Intermediate Peak (kW): As above, anchor to "Intermediate Peak (kW)".
- Demand - On Peak (kW): As above, anchor to "On Peak (kW)".
- Total Metered (kW): Anchor with "Total Metered" or "Total Demand Use". If not found, return NaN.
- Demand - Total Billed (kW): Anchor with "Total Billed (kW)". Return NaN if not specified.
- Demand - kVA: Anchor with "Demand - kVA" or "kVA". Return NaN if not specified.
- Supply Charges($): Anchor with "Supply", "Supply Charges", or lines showing "Supply" and a currency amount. Capture value. Remove "$" sign when normalizing for numbers, keep for string fields.
- Product: For line charges, anchor with a recognizable label (such as "Transmission Dmd Chrg") in the charges breakdown/table.
- Description: Extract text adjacent to Product that details calculation (e.g., "28.20KW X $9.36000").
- Charges: Amount in the charges table corresponding to Product/Description. If not present, return NaN.
- amount: For each detailed charge (line item), anchor on Product row and capture adjacent amount. Use the number/string with or without currency symbol; remove "$" and "," for numeric field normalization.
- file_name: Use the current PDF file name.

Normalization:
- For dates, use "MM/DD/YY" format; pad years as needed. Accept "MM/DD/YYYY" and reduce to short year if required.
- For numbers/currency, remove commas and "$" for numeric and calculation fields. Store blank or missing/unknown fields as "NaN".

If tables or line item grids exist:
- Identify start by locating common column headers or charge sections (e.g., "Product", "Description", "$"/"Amount"), and end at totals or next section.
- Map columns left to right based on header positions; for each product/charge row, extract fields as above.

Always prefer the most prominent/latest value when labels/sections repeat (e.g. the first occurrence of "Account Number" near the statement date or customer name). Return missing/empty fields as "NaN".
